html:
PROS-R.BK: a distressed micro-cap contractor with a real but unproven Thailand data-center option

The old thesis is too promotional. Prosper Engineering is not visibly net cash, I found no reliable public evidence for an THB 8 billion hyperscale backlog, and the live equity value is nowhere near the USD 88 million scrape in the prompt. The investable thesis is better, stranger, and more asymmetric: PROS is a near-forgotten Thai M&E contractor priced at roughly THB 0.20, about THB 110 million market cap on 551.5 million shares, after a 50% to 80% share-price collapse, weak 2025 results, director financial-assistance disclosures, and a just-finished warrant overhang. That is the setup for a small, ugly, balance-sheet-constrained company that only needs to win a tiny slice of Thailand’s exploding data-center electrical and cooling work to become mispriced.

The company’s current financials explain why nobody wants it. SET/IR’s Q1 2026 snapshot shows only THB 118.5 million of total income, THB 639.5 million of assets, THB 95.9 million of equity, 7.05% gross margin, and 1.23% net margin (company IR). FY2025 was worse: revenue fell to about THB 830 million and net income was deeply negative after gross losses, while the SET factsheet still showed negative EBITDA and an EV above market cap because this is not a net-cash story (SET factsheet, StockAnalysis financials). On stale trailing numbers it is uninvestable.

But the forward question is not whether 2025 was bad. It is whether a company with a quoted equity value around THB 110 million can survive long enough to normalize revenue and attach itself to a construction cycle measured in hundreds of billions of baht. Thailand’s data-center pipeline is no longer a vague AI story. BOI approved seven data-center projects worth more than THB 96 billion in January 2026, then in May approved another giant wave led by TikTok and Bridge Data Centres, with total approved projects reported at THB 958 billion (Thairath, TNGlobal). Sector research lists AWS, TikTok, Galaxy Peak, Google, GDS, Digital Edge/B.Grimm, Equinix, Bridge, GSA, NEXTDC, STT GDC, Alibaba and others as active or planned Thailand data-center investors (LH Bank). This work is power, cooling, fire systems, water, controls, and mission-critical building engineering. That is exactly the market Prosper says it serves.

The variant perception is not that PROS is already a clean AI infrastructure compounder. It is that the market is valuing it as a broken small contractor with no option value, while the country around it is entering a multi-year M&E capacity squeeze. Data centers are constrained by grid access, high-voltage equipment, cooling, redundancy, and construction execution. A recent Thailand data-center power-grid warning framed the bottleneck as delivery of reliable megawatts, not just tenant demand (Nation Thailand). Even if Prosper is not the lead contractor on marquee hyperscale campuses, subcontracted electrical, cooling, fire protection, and building systems work can be material relative to a THB 110 million equity value.

The math is the attraction. If Prosper only returns to a THB 900 million to THB 1.1 billion revenue base and earns a normal 2.5% to 3.5% net margin, it can generate THB 25 million to THB 38 million of net income. That is a 3x to 4.5x forward P/E at the current quote. If it captures even THB 1.5 billion of higher-spec M&E work over a year or two and earns 4% net, that is THB 60 million of earnings, more than half the current market cap. The bull case does not require Prosper to dominate Thailand data-center construction. It requires survival, better project selection, and modest participation.

The finished PROS-W1 process matters because it removes a messy trading distraction. The warrant’s last trading day was April 20, 2026, final exercise date was May 14, and delisting was May 15 (SET). That does not magically recapitalize the company, especially with the stock so far below prior levels, but it cleans up one overhang. The more important overhang is the April 2026 stream of announcements around director financial assistance, covenant compliance, capital reduction/registration, and capital increase. That is the real bear evidence. This is not a pristine balance sheet. It is a refinancing and execution turnaround.

The social/retail context is also favorable in a narrow way: there is plenty of retail heat around Thailand data centers and AI infrastructure, but almost no English-language retail sponsorship for Prosper itself. The stock is too small, too ugly, and too hard to screen. That means there is no thematic premium embedded. If Q2 or Q3 2026 shows revenue acceleration, gross margin staying above 7% to 8%, no new emergency financing, and any credible data-center or high-spec M&E contract award, the narrative can change quickly because the float and market cap are tiny.

What breaks the thesis is simple: if revenue remains stuck near Q1 levels, gross margin stays in the mid-single digits, director loans keep being extended, or a discounted private placement heavily dilutes minorities without creating bidding capacity, PROS is a value trap. A single bad fixed-price project can erase the equity. This is not a downside-protected net cash play. It is a small distressed contractor with a potentially very valuable local demand shock.

Current price used: THB 0.20, based on the latest delayed StockAnalysis history showing THB 0.20 on May 11, 2026, with SET showing THB 0.22 on May 9 (history, SET price).

Bear target: THB 0.08, down 60%. Covenant pressure, dilution, weak margins, and no data-center capture leave common equity impaired.

Base target: THB 0.50, up 150%. Revenue normalizes toward THB 1 billion, net income reaches THB 30 million to THB 35 million, and the market pays 8x earnings after the warrant and financing overhangs clear.

Bull target: THB 1.20, up 500%. Prosper wins meaningful high-spec M&E packages in the Thailand data-center buildout, revenue moves above THB 1.5 billion, net margin reaches 4% to 5%, and the market values THB 70 million to THB 90 million of earnings at 7x to 9x despite micro-cap risk.
